_meta.name: questions
_meta.title: Canonical corpus — questions we teach the AI to ask
_meta.what: The cognitive/design heuristics a good thinker asks themselves while working. This is the SINGLE canonical source; consumers (Igor's reading extractor, skills, future training) draw from here rather than hardcoding their own copy.
_meta.why: Akien asks these constantly while designing ('how might I do this better? could I find an easier way?'). The goal is to teach AI to ask them too — as reading-extraction lenses, as node-construction prompts, and as training signal. (2026-06-25, forming design — node-construction insight pending.)

distinct_from.Q-xxx open-questions: RETIRED parking-lot experiment (the /question skill). These are NOT project-specific things-not-yet-decided.
distinct_from.CP1-CP6 core values: Separate frozen contract in diagnostic_base/core_values.py — referenced, not duplicated here.

_meta.seeded_from: unseen_university/devices/igor/tools/ebook_reader.py :: _INTERP_CANDIDATES (the PROC_HEURISTIC_* entries). That hardcoded list should later READ from this file (Igor wiring — deferred, igor = last priority).
_meta.status: seed v1 — open to reshaping once the node-construction insight lands

questions.question: If I did this differently, could I find an easier way? How might I do this better?
questions.lens: optimization & simplification — challenge the current approach, seek a simpler/better path
questions.added: 2026-06-25 (Akien — the designer question he noticed himself asking constantly)

questions.question: What's the first thing I say?
Which choice aligns with who I'd most like to be?
What looks like it would fit there?
How could we get around that?
How will humans screw this up?
